user_prompt: A user requested deletion of all their data. Generate the DSAR-deletion checklist including which systems to scrub.
actual_output:
# Data Subject Access Request (DSAR) — Deletion Checklist

**Disclaimer:** This template assumes a lawful deletion request under GDPR Articles 17 (right to erasure) or 21 (objection to processing). Verify the legal basis for deletion before proceeding — some data may have retention obligations (tax, contract, legal hold).

---

## Pre-Deletion Verification

- [ ] **Confirm request legitimacy**
  - Verify requestor identity (email confirmation, ID match if high-risk)
  - Document request date and method (email, form submission, etc.)
  - Check for legal holds, litigation, or regulatory investigation affecting this user

- [ ] **Assess deletion scope**
  - Is this a full erasure or selective deletion (e.g., marketing data only)?
  - Are there lawful grounds to retain some data? (contract performance, legal obligation, legitimate interest with balancing test)
  - Document any data that cannot be deleted and the legal basis for retention

- [ ] **Set 30-day response deadline**
  - Calculate deadline: request date + 30 calendar days
  - Flag for escalation if deadline is <5 business days away

---

## System Inventory & Deletion Checklist

### Production Systems

**Primary Database / CRM**
- [ ] Identify all records linked to user ID / email
- [ ] Soft delete or hard delete? (Document decision: soft = privacy-preserving, hard = irreversible)
- [ ] Verify cascading deletes (orders, transactions, preferences tied to user)
- [ ] Confirm deletion in transaction logs (if applicable)
- [ ] **Responsible team:** [Engineering/Database Admin]
- [ ] **Completion date:** ___________

**User Authentication System**
- [ ] Delete account credentials, password hashes, session tokens
- [ ] Remove from SSO/OAuth provider if applicable
- [ ] Revoke API keys or access tokens
- [ ] **Responsible team:** [Security/Identity Management]
- [ ] **Completion date:** ___________

**Analytics & Tracking**
- [ ] Google Analytics: Delete user ID from property, suppress historical data if possible
- [ ] Mixpanel / Amplitude / Segment: Delete user profile and event history
- [ ] Hotjar / session recording tools: Delete session replays
- [ ] Email tracking pixels: Remove from sent emails (prospective only; past opens cannot be untracked)
- [ ] **Responsible team:** [Product/Analytics]
- [ ] **Completion date:** ___________

**Email & Communication Systems**
- [ ] Delete from email marketing platform (Mailchimp, HubSpot, Klaviyo, etc.)
- [ ] Remove from mailing lists and segments
- [ ] Delete email history if stored separately (support tickets, newsletters)
- [ ] **Responsible team:** [Marketing/Support]
- [ ] **Completion date:** ___________

**Cloud Storage & File Systems**
- [ ] AWS S3 / Google Cloud Storage / Azure: Delete user-uploaded files and backups
- [ ] Shared drives (Google Drive, OneDrive): Remove user's files and access
- [ ] Backup systems: Verify deletion in backup retention schedule (may require waiting for backup expiration)
- [ ] **Responsible team:** [Infrastructure/DevOps]
- [ ] **Completion date:** ___________

**Payment & Billing Systems**
- [ ] Stripe / PayPal / Square: Anonymize customer records (may not fully delete due to PCI-DSS/tax requirements)
- [ ] Invoice history: Retain for tax/legal purposes but anonymize PII where possible
- [ ] Subscription records: Cancel active subscriptions, delete payment methods
- [ ] **Responsible team:** [Finance/Billing]
- [ ] **Completion date:** ___________

**Customer Support & Ticketing**
- [ ] Zendesk / Jira Service Desk / Freshdesk: Delete or anonymize support tickets
- [ ] Chat logs (Intercom, Drift): Delete conversation history
- [ ] Phone/call recordings: Delete if stored
- [ ] **Responsible team:** [Customer Support]
- [ ] **Completion date:** ___________

### Secondary Systems

**Third-Party Integrations & Processors**
- [ ] Slack (if user data synced): Request deletion from workspace
- [ ] Zapier / Make / IFTTT: Delete user data from automation logs
- [ ] Webhooks / API integrations: Notify connected services to delete user data
- [ ] **Responsible team:** [Integration/API Team]
- [ ] **Completion date:** ___________

**Data Warehouses & BI Tools**
- [ ] Snowflake / BigQuery / Redshift: Delete user records from warehouse
- [ ] Tableau / Looker / Power BI: Verify user data is removed from cached datasets
- [ ] **Responsible team:** [Data Engineering]
- [ ] **Completion date:** ___________

**Logs & Monitoring Systems**
- [ ] Application logs (CloudWatch, Datadog, New Relic): Purge user-identifying logs
- [ ] Server access logs: Delete or anonymize IP/user agent data
- [ ] Error tracking (Sentry, Rollbar): Remove user context from error reports
- [ ] **Responsible team:** [DevOps/Security]
- [ ] **Completion date:** ___________

**Backup & Disaster Recovery**
- [ ] Identify backup retention policy (e.g., 30-day rolling backups)
- [ ] Confirm deletion will propagate to all backup copies
- [ ] Document when oldest backup containing user data will expire
- [ ] **Responsible team:** [Infrastructure]
- [ ] **Completion date:** ___________

---

## Special Considerations

### Legal Holds & Litigation
- [ ] Is user data subject to litigation hold or regulatory investigation?
- [ ] If yes, deletion may be suspended — document legal basis for retention
- [ ] Notify legal team before proceeding

### Contractual Obligations
- [ ] Does contract require data retention (e.g., SaaS terms, service agreement)?
- [ ] If yes, document retention period and notify user of limitation
- [ ] Example: "We will delete your data within 30 days, except transaction records retained for 7 years per tax law"

### Anonymization vs. Deletion
- [ ] Can data be anonymized instead of deleted? (Irreversible removal of identifiers)
- [ ] Anonymized data is no longer personal data under GDPR — may be retained indefinitely
- [ ] Example: Delete name/email but retain usage statistics as aggregate data

### Data Shared with Third Parties
- [ ] Identify all processors and recipients who received user data
- [ ] Send deletion requests to each processor (Article 17(2) obligation)
- [ ] Document processor responses and completion dates
- [ ] **Processors to notify:**
  - [ ] [Processor 1]: __________ | Response: __________ | Date: __________
  - [ ] [Processor 2]: __________ | Response: __________ | Date: __________

---

## Completion & Documentation

- [ ] **Deletion verification:** Confirm data is unrecoverable from all systems
  - Run test query: SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = [user_id] → should return 0 rows
  - Spot-check backup systems for user data absence

- [ ] **Audit trail:** Document all deletion actions
  - Who deleted? When? From which systems?
  - Maintain deletion log for 3 years (demonstrates compliance if audited)

- [ ] **User notification:** Send confirmation email
  - Template: "Your deletion request dated [DATE] has been completed. All personal data has been removed from our systems as of [DATE]. You may no longer access your account."
  - Include contact info for follow-up questions

- [ ] **Close DSAR ticket**
  - Mark request as "Completed" in DSAR tracking system
  - Attach deletion verification report

---

## Timeline & Accountability

| Phase | Owner | Deadline | Status |
|-------|-------|----------|--------|
| Verification & legal review | [DPO/Legal] | Day 2 | ☐ |
| Production database deletion | [Engineering] | Day 10 | ☐ |
| Third-party processor deletion | [Compliance] | Day 20 | ☐ |
| Backup expiration confirmation | [Infrastructure] | Day 25 | ☐ |
| User notification & closure | [Compliance] | Day 30 | ☐ |

---

## Risk Flags

🚩 **High Risk:** User data in litigation hold — escalate to legal immediately  
🚩 **Medium Risk:** Data in backups with 90+ day retention — deletion may
judge_reasoning: The chatbot provided a comprehensive and detailed DSAR deletion checklist that directly addressed the user's request while adhering to its persona and guidelines.
